0.adjustedReason: Chore PR #534 merged post-dispatch; rebased phase-0 diff baseline forward so integrity gate excludes chore commits (docs only).

followUpsMedium: SkillsAssembler.java grew to 396 lines (pre-existing 250-line violation worsened) — extract SkillsAssemblerPipeline in follow-up

Story Gherkin Cenario 2 supersession should be annotated for audit clarity
story-0047-0001.summary: Created _shared/ dir with 3 initial snippets + README, authored ADR-0011 (link-based shared-snippets strategy — slot 0006 taken by EPIC-0041), extended SkillsAssembler.assembleShared(), piloted pre-commit cluster carve-out, regen 17 profile goldens + 2 platform variants, added SharedSnippetsAssemblerTest (5 TPP) + Epic0047CompressionSmokeTest (51 params). mvn verify green: 4212 surefire + 810 failsafe + JaCoCo. Note: Specialist Review (Phase 4) and Tech Lead Review (Phase 7) SKIPPED by subagent (protocol deviation — x-epic-implement dispatching a post-hoc review subagent).

story-0047-0003.summary: SkillSizeLinter (RULE-047-04) CI guard-rail shipped with baseline-exemption (25 existing oversized SKILL.md tolerated via audits/skill-size-baseline.txt). SkillCorpusSizeAudit (RULE-047-07) soft-warn default; hard-fail opt-in via -Dskill.corpus.audit.enforce=true after 0002/0004 close the 20,197-line gap. 5 TDD cycles, 8 atomic commits, mvn verify green.
story-0047-0003.coverageNote: Line 91% below story DoD 95% (gap = 6 defensive UncheckedIOException catches; not triggerable without mock NIO). Global Rule 05 threshold (85/80) satisfied.
story-0047-0003.reviewMode: inline-RULE-012-graceful-degradation (Agent tool unavailable in subagent session; less independent than PR #535's out-of-session post-hoc review)
followUpsHigh: Rule 14 tension: SkillSizeLinter.java (and SkillCorpusSizeAudit) placed in src/main/java/ violates Rule 14 (CI validation gates forbidden in Java production code). Rule 14 landed same day as story dispatch in commit 6b2470212 (epic base). Tech Lead GO (41/45) treated as non-blocking follow-up. Fix options: (X) relocate to src/test/java/ following HexagonalArchitectureBaselineAudit precedent; (Y) carve quality/ exception into Rule 14. User decision at PR #536 merge time: AUTO_MERGE — follow-up PR will address relocation.
followUpsMedium: Coverage line 91% misses story DoD 95% target (gap is defensive catches); consider refactoring error handling to be mockable in follow-up
Review was inline (RULE-012 graceful degradation) rather than via independent post-hoc subagent — consider dispatching one for parity with PR #535 quality signal

story-0047-0002.retryReason: First dispatch aborted Phase 0 due to scope mismatch in orchestrator prompt (targets were top orchestrators, authoritative story file targets were 5 ex-Slim-Mode skills). Subagent correctly refused per RULE-001. Re-dispatched with corrected prompt.
story-0047-0002.summary: 5 pilot skills flipped to slim contract per ADR-0012 (ADR-0007 slot was taken → next available). Combined 2,988 → 565 source lines (−81%, 44% under ≤1,000 DoD budget). 7 atomic task commits + 2 review artifact commits. mvn verify BUILD SUCCESS (4237 tests, 0 failures). Specialist review 39/40 (inline RULE-012 — 5 of 8 specialist domains N/A for doc-refactor with zero new Java). Tech Lead review 45/45 (PERFECT SCORE).
story-0047-0002.reviewMode: inline-RULE-012-graceful-degradation (rationale: 5 of 8 specialist domains N/A for doc-only refactor; Security/Performance/Database/Observability/DevOps/API/Event skipped; QA + Spec retained)
story-0047-0002.findingsSummary: 1 finding — pre-existing: LazyKpLoadingTest$LifecyclePrompts (story-0030-0003) and ApiFirstPhaseTest$LifecyclePhaseContent (story-0017-0007) adapted to read SKILL.md + references/full-protocol.md concatenation. Non-functional test adaptation preserving original invariants; correct architectural response to slim/full split per ADR-0012.

techLeadDeductions: Section K (TDD Process) −2pts: single consolidated commit (orchestrator recovery after subagent mid-lifecycle exit) vs ideal per-task atomic commits. Spirit met because story plan §7.3 pre-authorized test-after-no-new-code for doc-heavy refactor; letter not met. Not a NO-GO.
story-0047-0004.recoveryPlaybookRecommendation: Tech Lead recommendation for future: add 'force atomic per-task commits when recovering from subagent exit' to the recovery playbook (applies only to non-doc stories; doc-heavy refactors remain exempt per plan pre-authorization).

baselineExemptionUpdates.entriesKept: 20 remaining (core/** SKILL.md files > 500 lines that are not in this story's scope)

story-0047-0004.protocolDeviation: Implementing subagent exited after Phase 4 specialist review without performing Phase 6 (commit+PR) or Phase 7 (Tech Lead review). Epic orchestrator finalized commit+push+PR creation (commit a0277a44d on feat/story-0047-0004-kp-compression-sweep); Tech Lead review delegated to an independent post-hoc subagent. Similar pattern used successfully for story-0047-0001 post-hoc review (PR #535).

finalSummary.corpusTargetGapNote: Epic delivered its scope (framework structural enforcement + pilot compression) but the aspirational -40% target of ≤30,115 lines is NOT achievable from this epic alone. Remaining ~13,200-line gap requires follow-up compression epics (e.g., applying ADR-0012 flipped-orientation pattern to the remaining 7 orchestrators > 500 lines currently baselined in audits/skill-size-baseline.txt). Documented in ADR-0012 §Migration Path and in Tech Lead review §Follow-ups.

recoveryPlaybookAdditions: For non-doc stories, force atomic per-task commits when recovering from subagent mid-lifecycle exit (Tech Lead 0004)

If orchestrator prompt scope conflicts with on-disk authoritative artifacts (story file / tasks file / IMPLEMENTATION-MAP), ABORT and surface — do NOT proceed on unclear contract (subagent 0002 first dispatch)
ADR slot validation: always `ls adr/` before writing a new ADR; if planned slot is taken, use next available and update story §3.2 (same pattern used for 0006→0011 and 0007→0012)
